bids.reasoning: The opponent opened with their best offer (all valueD), which gives me minimum utility (0.2 across all issues = 0.2 total). This is very early (t=0.0), so I should counter with my ideal bid to anchor high. My best offer is all valueA, giving me utility of 0.4*1 + 0.3*1 + 0.2*1 + 0.1*1 = 1.0. I'll start firm at my maximum utility position.

The opponent is stubbornly repeating their all-valueD offer (utility ~0.2 for me), which is very low. We're only at t=0.02, so there's no time pressure yet. I should stay near my optimal bid but perhaps make a tiny concession to signal willingness to negotiate. I'll keep issueA at valueA (highest weight) and issueB at valueA, but concede slightly on issueC and issueD (lower weight issues) to show some flexibility.

The opponent is stubbornly holding at their best offer (all valueD) which gives me very low utility (0.2 across all issues). It's very early (t=0.04), so I should stay firm near my ideal. I'll make a small concession on the least important issues (issueC and issueD) while holding strong on issueA and issueB which have the highest weights. I'll try valueB on issueC and issueD to signal willingness to negotiate without giving up much.

The opponent has shifted significantly in their latest offer - they moved issueB from valueD to valueA and issueC from valueD to valueA, which is a big concession. This suggests they care most about issueA and issueD (still holding at valueD and valueC respectively). Since issueA is my highest weight issue (0.4), I should hold firm there. The opponent seems to value issueA: valueD strongly. I'll concede slightly on lower-weight issues (issueC, issueD) while maintaining my position on issueA and issueB since those are my top priorities. We're still very early (t=0.06), so I don't need to make large concessions yet.

The opponent is still very early in the negotiation and their offers have been inconsistent - mostly valueD across issues but with some variation. Their latest offer (t=0.08) shows issueA: valueB, issueB: valueD, issueC: valueA, issueD: valueB, which gives me utility of 0.4*0.73 + 0.3*0.20 + 0.2*1.00 + 0.1*0.73 = 0.292 + 0.06 + 0.20 + 0.073 = 0.625. It's still very early (t=0.08), so I should stay near my top utility while making small concessions. I'll keep issueA and issueB at valueA (my highest weight issues) and concede slightly on issueC and issueD to signal flexibility.

The opponent is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.1) and their offers have been inconsistent - sometimes all valueD, sometimes mixed. Their latest offer (issueA: valueC, issueB: valueD, issueC: valueD, issueD: valueD) gives me very low utility (0.4*0.47 + 0.3*0.20 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.1*0.20 = 0.188+0.06+0.04+0.02 = 0.308). I should stay firm on my high-weight issues (issueA, issueB) while making small concessions on lower-weight issues. I'll keep issueA at valueA and issueB at valueA, and concede slightly on issueC and issueD to show willingness to negotiate.

The opponent is not conceding meaningfully - they keep offering all valueD (worst for me) with occasional variations. Their latest offer (issueA: valueC, issueB: valueD, issueC: valueD, issueD: valueD) gives me very low utility (~0.47*0.4 + 0.20*0.3 + 0.20*0.2 + 0.20*0.1 = 0.188+0.06+0.04+0.02 = 0.308). We're still very early (t=0.12), so I should hold firm near my top bids. I'll maintain my strong position on issueA and issueB (highest weights) while making a small concession on issueC and issueD to signal willingness to negotiate.
